title: Revision and Practice — Days 1–9
description: 500 mixed revision questions covering all 9 days of grammar

categories.title: Error Detection
categories.description: Find and correct the mistake in each sentence

questions.hindi: नीचे दिए वाक्य में गलती ढूंढो: 'She don't eat meat.'
questions.english: She doesn't eat meat.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'He go to office every day.'
questions.english: He goes to office every day.
questions.explanation: Third person singular (He/She/It) के साथ verb में 's' जोड़ते हैं।
questions.wrongSentence: He go to office every day.
questions.correctSentence: He goes to office every day.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'They is coming tomorrow.'
questions.english: They are coming tomorrow.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'I am having a car.'
questions.english: I have a car.
questions.explanation: Possession (मालिकाना) के लिए simple present 'have' use करते हैं, progressive नहीं।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'This books are mine.'
questions.english: These books are mine.
questions.explanation: Plural noun 'books' के साथ 'these' आता है, 'this' नहीं।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'She is knowing the answer.'
questions.english: She knows the answer.
questions.explanation: Mental state verbs (know, understand, believe) continuous tense में नहीं आते।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'There was many people at the party.'
questions.english: There were many people at the party.
questions.explanation: Plural noun 'people' के साथ 'were' आता है।
questions.wrongSentence: There was many people at the party.
questions.correctSentence: There were many people at the party.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'He have completed the work.'
questions.english: He has completed the work.
questions.explanation: 'He' (third person singular) के साथ 'has' आता है।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'By tomorrow I will have finish the report.'
questions.english: By tomorrow I will have finished the report.
questions.explanation: 'Will have' के बाद past participle (finished) आता है।
questions.wrongSentence: By tomorrow I will have finish the report.
questions.correctSentence: By tomorrow I will have finished the report.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'That are my friends.'
questions.english: Those are my friends.
questions.explanation: Plural noun के साथ 'those' आता है, 'that' नहीं।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'Please to sit down.'
questions.english: Please sit down.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'I am understanding this problem.'
questions.english: I understand this problem.
questions.explanation: 'Understand' stative verb है — progressive tense नहीं बनता।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'She had went to Mumbai last year.'
questions.english: She had gone to Mumbai last year.
questions.explanation: 'Had' के बाद past participle (gone) आता है, simple past (went) नहीं।
questions.wrongSentence: She had went to Mumbai last year.
questions.correctSentence: She had gone to Mumbai last year.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'He don't have any money.'
questions.english: He doesn't have any money.
questions.explanation: Third person singular के साथ 'doesn't' use होता है।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'There is a lot of problems.'
questions.english: There are a lot of problems.
questions.wrongSentence: There is a lot of problems.
questions.correctSentence: There are a lot of problems.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'We was waiting for you.'
questions.english: We were waiting for you.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'These is a good idea.'
questions.english: This is a good idea.
questions.explanation: Singular 'idea' के साथ 'this' और 'is' आता है।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'She have been working here since 5 years.'
questions.english: She has been working here for 5 years.
questions.explanation: 'She' के साथ 'has', और duration के साथ 'for' use होता है।
questions.wrongSentence: She have been working here since 5 years.
questions.correctSentence: She has been working here for 5 years.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'Don't to worry about it.'
questions.english: Don't worry about it.
questions.explanation: Negative imperative में 'Don't + base verb' use होता है।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'He were absent yesterday.'
questions.english: He was absent yesterday.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'I has completed my project.'
questions.english: I have completed my project.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'Those chair is broken.'
questions.english: That chair is broken.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'By next week, she will have submit the form.'
questions.english: By next week, she will have submitted the form.
questions.explanation: 'Will have' के बाद past participle (submitted) आता है।
questions.wrongSentence: By next week, she will have submit the form.
questions.correctSentence: By next week, she will have submitted the form.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'There were a dog in the garden.'
questions.english: There was a dog in the garden.
questions.wrongSentence: There were a dog in the garden.
questions.correctSentence: There was a dog in the garden.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'My name Rahul is.'
questions.english: My name is Rahul.
questions.explanation: English में SVO order होता है — Subject + Be Verb + Object.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'He have a meeting at 3 PM.'
questions.english: He has a meeting at 3 PM.
questions.explanation: Third person singular के साथ 'has' आता है।
questions.wrongSentence: He have a meeting at 3 PM.
questions.correctSentence: He has a meeting at 3 PM.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'I am belong to Delhi.'
questions.english: I belong to Delhi.
questions.explanation: 'Belong' stative verb है — progressive form नहीं बनता।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'Before I arrived, they have already left.'
questions.english: Before I arrived, they had already left.
questions.explanation: पहले complete हुई action के लिए past perfect (had + V3) use होता है।
questions.wrongSentence: Before I arrived, they have already left.
questions.correctSentence: Before I arrived, they had already left.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'An university is near my house.'
questions.english: A university is near my house.
questions.explanation: 'University' की आवाज़ 'य' (consonant sound) से शुरू होती है, इसलिए 'a' use होता है।
questions.wrongSentence: An university is near my house.
questions.correctSentence: A university is near my house.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'She doesn't knows how to cook.'
questions.english: She doesn't know how to cook.
questions.explanation: 'Doesn't' के बाद base form of verb आता है, 'knows' नहीं।
questions.wrongSentence: She doesn't knows how to cook.
questions.correctSentence: She doesn't know how to cook.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'These informations are incorrect.'
questions.english: This information is incorrect.
questions.explanation: 'Information' uncountable noun है — plural नहीं होता।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'There will be no problem if you will follow the rules.'
questions.english: There will be no problem if you follow the rules.
questions.wrongSentence: There will be no problem if you will follow the rules.
questions.correctSentence: There will be no problem if you follow the rules.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'He is my cousin brother.'
questions.english: He is my cousin.
questions.explanation: 'Cousin' already male or female होता है — 'brother/sister' नहीं जोड़ते।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'I will have call you by evening.'
questions.english: I will have called you by evening.
questions.explanation: 'Will have' के बाद past participle (called) आता है।
questions.wrongSentence: I will have call you by evening.
questions.correctSentence: I will have called you by evening.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'Those people was very helpful.'
questions.english: Those people were very helpful.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'She can speaks English well.'
questions.english: She can speak English well.
questions.explanation: Modal verb (can) के बाद base form of verb आता है।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'There is many options available.'
questions.english: There are many options available.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'He had ate his lunch before the meeting.'
questions.english: He had eaten his lunch before the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Had' के बाद past participle (eaten) आता है, simple past (ate) नहीं।
questions.wrongSentence: He had ate his lunch before the meeting.
questions.correctSentence: He had eaten his lunch before the meeting.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'I am work in IT company.'
questions.english: I work in an IT company.
questions.explanation: Simple present में 'am work' नहीं बनता — 'am working' या सिर्फ 'work' होता है।
questions.wrongSentence: I am work in IT company.
questions.correctSentence: I work in an IT company.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'By the time he arrives, I will have been finish.'
questions.english: By the time he arrives, I will have finished.
questions.explanation: Future perfect में 'will have + past participle' होता है।
questions.wrongSentence: By the time he arrives, I will have been finish.
questions.correctSentence: By the time he arrives, I will have finished.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'These is my pen.'
questions.english: This is my pen.
questions.explanation: Singular 'pen' के साथ 'this' और 'is' आता है।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'He is 25 years old aged.'
questions.english: He is 25 years old.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'Do not to make noise in the library.'
questions.english: Do not make noise in the library.
questions.explanation: Negative imperative में 'Do not + base verb' होता है।
questions.wrongSentence: Do not to make noise in the library.
questions.correctSentence: Do not make noise in the library.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'She has already went home.'
questions.english: She has already gone home.
questions.explanation: 'Has' के बाद past participle (gone) आता है।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'I gives him a book every week.'
questions.english: I give him a book every week.
questions.explanation: 'I' के साथ simple present में verb में 's' नहीं जोड़ते।
questions.wrongSentence: I gives him a book every week.
questions.correctSentence: I give him a book every week.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'That problems need to be solved.'
questions.english: Those problems need to be solved.
questions.wrongSentence: That problems need to be solved.
questions.correctSentence: Those problems need to be solved.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'There have been a mistake.'
questions.english: There has been a mistake.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'We had submitted the report already before the deadline.'
questions.english: We had already submitted the report before the deadline.
questions.explanation: 'Already' usually verb के पहले आता है — adverb placement।
questions.wrongSentence: We had submitted the report already before the deadline.
questions.correctSentence: We had already submitted the report before the deadline.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'He is my good friend since college.'
questions.english: He has been my good friend since college.
questions.explanation: 'Since' के साथ present perfect (has been) आता है।
questions.wrongSentence: He is my good friend since college.
questions.correctSentence: He has been my good friend since college.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'Please don't disturbs me during the meeting.'
questions.english: Please don't disturb me during the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Don't' के बाद base form (disturb) आता है।
questions.wrongSentence: Please don't disturbs me during the meeting.
questions.correctSentence: Please don't disturb me during the meeting.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'I am from Mumbai city.'
questions.english: I am from Mumbai.
questions.explanation: City name के साथ 'city' repeat नहीं करते।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'They has not replied yet.'
questions.english: They have not replied yet.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'There will be many peoples attending the seminar.'
questions.english: There will be many people attending the seminar.
questions.wrongSentence: There will be many peoples attending the seminar.
questions.correctSentence: There will be many people attending the seminar.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'She was not knowing about the meeting.'
questions.english: She did not know about the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Know' stative verb है — past में 'did not know' use होता है।
questions.wrongSentence: She was not knowing about the meeting.
questions.correctSentence: She did not know about the meeting.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'He done his homework.'
questions.english: He did his homework. / He has done his homework.
questions.explanation: Simple past में 'did' और present perfect में 'has done' use होता है।

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'Please to give me your attention.'
questions.english: Please give me your attention.
questions.explanation: Imperative में 'Please + base verb' — 'to' नहीं लगाते।
questions.wrongSentence: Please to give me your attention.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'By Friday they will have complete 10 modules.'
questions.english: By Friday they will have completed 10 modules.
questions.explanation: 'Will have' के बाद past participle (completed) आता है।
questions.wrongSentence: By Friday they will have complete 10 modules.
questions.correctSentence: By Friday they will have completed 10 modules.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'That cars are very expensive.'
questions.english: Those cars are very expensive.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'I never have seen such a beautiful place.'
questions.english: I have never seen such a beautiful place.
questions.explanation: 'Never' auxiliary verb के बाद आता है — 'have never seen'।
questions.wrongSentence: I never have seen such a beautiful place.
questions.correctSentence: I have never seen such a beautiful place.

questions.hindi: गलती ढूंढो: 'There is no chairs in the room.'
questions.english: There are no chairs in the room.
questions.wrongSentence: There is no chairs in the room.
questions.correctSentence: There are no chairs in the room.

categories.title: Sentence Transformation
categories.description: Change positive to negative, statement to question, or tense

questions.hindi: Positive से Negative बनाओ: 'She eats lunch at 1 PM.'
questions.english: She does not eat lunch at 1 PM.
questions.transformed: She does not eat lunch at 1 PM.

questions.hindi: Statement से Question बनाओ: 'He is a software engineer.'
questions.english: Is he a software engineer?
questions.transformed: Is he a software engineer?

questions.hindi: Present से Past बनाओ: 'There are five people in the team.'
questions.english: There were five people in the team.
questions.original: There are five people in the team.
questions.transformed: There were five people in the team.

questions.hindi: Positive से Negative बनाओ: 'They have completed the project.'
questions.english: They have not completed the project.
questions.transformed: They have not completed the project.

questions.hindi: Statement से Question बनाओ: 'She has a meeting today.'
questions.english: Does she have a meeting today?
questions.transformed: Does she have a meeting today?

questions.hindi: Simple Present से Present Perfect बनाओ: 'I write the report.'
questions.english: I have written the report.
questions.transformed: I have written the report.

questions.hindi: Positive से Negative बनाओ: 'This is the right answer.'
questions.english: This is not the right answer.
questions.transformed: This is not the right answer.

questions.hindi: Statement से Question बनाओ: 'There was a problem in the system.'

questions.original: There was a problem in the system.
questions.transformed: Was there a problem in the system?

questions.hindi: Past Perfect से Future Perfect बनाओ: 'He had finished the task.'
questions.english: He will have finished the task.

questions.hindi: Singular से Plural बनाओ: 'This book is interesting.'

questions.hindi: Statement से Question बनाओ: 'They have submitted the report.'
questions.english: Have they submitted the report?

questions.hindi: Present से Future Perfect बनाओ: 'I finish the course.'
questions.english: I will have finished the course.

questions.hindi: Affirmative Imperative से Negative बनाओ: 'Open the window.'

questions.hindi: Positive से Negative बनाओ: 'There will be a presentation tomorrow.'

questions.hindi: Singular से Plural बनाओ: 'That document was important.'

questions.hindi: Statement से Question बनाओ: 'She had already left when I arrived.'

questions.transformed: Had she already left when I arrived?

questions.hindi: Positive से Negative बनाओ: 'I have visited Delhi twice.'

questions.hindi: Past से Present Perfect बनाओ: 'She wrote the email.'

questions.hindi: Statement से Question बनाओ: 'He will have completed 10 years by 2025.'

questions.hindi: Positive से Negative बनाओ: 'We are ready for the meeting.'

questions.hindi: Negative Imperative से Positive बनाओ: 'Don't be late.'

questions.hindi: Positive से Negative बनाओ: 'He has two years of experience.'

questions.explanation: 'She' के साथ 'has had' (present perfect) आता है।

questions.hindi: There ___ (will be/will are) a team outing next week.

questions.hindi: They ___ (hadn't/haven't) received the package yet.

questions.hindi: Before the manager arrived, the team ___ already ___ the plan. (had/prepared)

I ___ (am/have) a 5-year plan for my career.

questions.hindi: By the end of this quarter, we ___ launched the product. (will have/had)
questions.explanation: Future perfect के लिए 'will have + V3'।

questions.hindi: ___ (Is/Are) those files ready for upload?
She ___ (hadn't/hasn't) replied to my email yet.
questions.explanation: 'Yet' present perfect के साथ use होता है।

questions.hindi: There ___ (is/are) many reasons for this decision.

questions.explanation: Past action के पहले complete हुई action — past perfect।

questions.hindi: ___ (These/This) data needs to be verified.

questions.hindi: By 2030, she ___ worked here for 10 years. (will have/would)

questions.hindi: By the deadline, they ___ submitted all modules. (will have/had)

questions.hindi: She ___ (doesn't/don't) have access to the portal.

questions.hindi: ___ (That/Those) is the manager's desk.

questions.explanation: Past time reference के साथ 'had' (past perfect)।

categories.title: Common Indian English Errors to Fix
categories.description: Common mistakes made by Indian English speakers — corrected

questions.correct: I have a car.
questions.hindi: मेरे पास एक कार है।
questions.rule: Possession के लिए progressive form नहीं।

questions.correct: She knows him.
questions.hindi: वह उसे जानती है।

questions.correct: I understand this.
questions.hindi: मैं यह समझता हूँ।

questions.correct: He has a headache.
questions.hindi: उसके सिर में दर्द है।

questions.correct: I don't remember.
questions.hindi: मुझे याद नहीं है।

questions.correct: She is my cousin.
questions.hindi: वह मेरी कज़िन है।

questions.wrong: He is my good friend since 2010.
questions.correct: He has been my good friend since 2010.
questions.hindi: वह 2010 से मेरा अच्छा दोस्त है।

questions.correct: What is your name?
questions.hindi: तुम्हारा नाम क्या है?
questions.rule: 'Good name' Indian English — just 'name' use करते हैं।

questions.wrong: I did the same mistake again.
questions.correct: I made the same mistake again.
questions.hindi: मैंने फिर वही गलती की।

questions.correct: She took an exam.
questions.hindi: उसने परीक्षा दी।

questions.correct: I have been working for 3 years.
questions.hindi: मैं 3 साल से काम कर रहा हूँ।
questions.rule: Duration के साथ 'for' + present perfect continuous।

questions.correct: He is better.
questions.hindi: वह बेहतर है।
questions.rule: 'More better' double comparative — 'better' ही काफी है।

questions.correct: Please do this / Could you please…
questions.hindi: कृपया यह करो।
questions.rule: 'Do one thing' — typical Indian English, say 'please do this'।

questions.wrong: I have passed out from IIT.
questions.correct: I have graduated from IIT.
questions.hindi: मैंने IIT से ग्रेजुएशन किया है।

questions.correct: She is very tired.
questions.hindi: वह बहुत थकी हुई है।

questions.correct: I will revert. / I will get back to you.
questions.hindi: मैं जवाब दूँगा।

questions.wrong: We need to prepone the meeting.
questions.correct: We need to reschedule the meeting to an earlier time.
questions.hindi: हमें मीटिंग पहले करनी है।

questions.correct: I agree with you.
questions.hindi: मैं तुमसे सहमत हूँ।

questions.correct: Please take care of this. / Please proceed accordingly.
questions.hindi: कृपया ज़रूरी कदम उठाओ।
questions.rule: 'Do the needful' — old formal, use clearer expressions।

questions.correct: He was the one who told me. / He just told me.
questions.hindi: उसी ने मुझे बताया था।
questions.rule: 'Only' as emphasis at end — not standard।

questions.correct: I have a question.
questions.hindi: मेरे मन में एक सवाल है।

questions.correct: She is my junior.
questions.hindi: वह मुझसे junior है।

questions.wrong: My English is not up to the mark.
questions.correct: My English needs improvement.
questions.hindi: मेरी अंग्रेज़ी में सुधार की ज़रूरत है।

questions.wrong: I am going to give a test tomorrow.
questions.correct: I am going to take a test tomorrow.
questions.hindi: मैं कल परीक्षा दूँगा।

questions.correct: Discuss this topic.
questions.hindi: इस विषय पर चर्चा करो।

questions.correct: I need to inform/notify you.
questions.hindi: मुझे तुम्हें बताना है।

questions.wrong: I am in a meeting. Can you call back later?
questions.correct: I'm in a meeting. Can I call you back?
questions.hindi: मैं मीटिंग में हूँ। क्या मैं बाद में call कर सकता हूँ?
questions.rule: More natural: 'Can I call you back?' (more polite)।

questions.wrong: She is not well since two days.
questions.correct: She has not been well for two days.
questions.hindi: वह दो दिन से ठीक नहीं है।

questions.wrong: The answer is actually coming from this formula.
questions.correct: The answer comes from this formula.
questions.hindi: जवाब इस formula से आता है।
questions.rule: General truth — simple present, not progressive।

questions.wrong: Today I am not feeling to work.
questions.correct: I don't feel like working today.
questions.hindi: आज मेरा काम करने का मन नहीं है।

questions.wrong: I have been to Delhi 2 years back.
questions.correct: I went to Delhi 2 years ago.
questions.hindi: मैं 2 साल पहले दिल्ली गया था।
questions.rule: 'Ago' — specific past time; 'have been' doesn't go with 'ago'।

questions.correct: We have been here since morning.
questions.hindi: हम सुबह से यहाँ हैं।

questions.wrong: As per me, this is correct.
questions.correct: According to me, this is correct. / In my opinion, this is correct.
questions.hindi: मेरे अनुसार, यह सही है।
questions.rule: 'As per me' — not standard; use 'according to me'।

questions.wrong: He told me to give me his number.
questions.correct: He told me to give him my number.
questions.hindi: उसने मुझे अपना नंबर देने को कहा।

questions.wrong: I want to pursue MBA and then do service.
questions.correct: I want to pursue an MBA and then work.
questions.hindi: मैं MBA करना चाहता हूँ और फिर काम करना चाहता हूँ।
questions.rule: 'Do service' — not standard; 'work' or 'get a job'।

questions.wrong: Yesterday I have gone to the market.
questions.correct: Yesterday I went to the market.
questions.hindi: कल मैं बाज़ार गया था।
questions.rule: 'Yesterday' specific past — simple past, not present perfect।

questions.wrong: He is not in the station.
questions.correct: He is not at the station.
questions.hindi: वह स्टेशन पर नहीं है।

questions.wrong: I am not in a mood to talk.
questions.correct: I am not in the mood to talk.
questions.hindi: मेरा बात करने का मन नहीं है।

questions.wrong: She is knowing how to drive.
questions.correct: She knows how to drive.
questions.hindi: उसे गाड़ी चलानी आती है।

questions.correct: The project is in progress. / The project is underway.
questions.hindi: प्रोजेक्ट चल रहा है।

questions.correct: He has good knowledge.
questions.hindi: उसे अच्छी जानकारी है।

questions.wrong: I will be in the outstation.
questions.correct: I will be out of town. / I will be traveling.
questions.hindi: मैं बाहर जाऊँगा।

questions.wrong: Please send me the address of your home.
questions.correct: Please send me your home address.
questions.hindi: कृपया मुझे अपना घर का पता भेजो।
questions.rule: Natural English word order for compound noun।

questions.correct: I can't sleep. / I'm having trouble sleeping.
questions.hindi: मुझे नींद नहीं आ रही।

questions.correct: Let's give him a round of applause.
questions.hindi: उनके लिए तालियाँ बजाएँ।

questions.wrong: I am on leave from tomorrow.
questions.correct: I will be on leave from tomorrow.
questions.hindi: मैं कल से छुट्टी पर हूँगा।

questions.wrong: He is not attending the calls.
questions.correct: He is not answering the calls.
questions.hindi: वह कॉल नहीं उठा रहा।

questions.correct: She did not turn up.
questions.hindi: वह नहीं आई।

questions.wrong: He is fond of eating sweets a lot.
questions.correct: He is very fond of eating sweets.
questions.hindi: उसे मिठाई खाना बहुत पसंद है।
questions.rule: 'Very' before adjective, not 'a lot' at end।

questions.correct: I am Priya. / My name is Priya.
questions.rule: 'Myself' used for emphasis — not for introduction।
